Verified the final source and ran `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/`, which passed with 20 test files and 60 tests green.","files_changed":["reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/AudioTrack.tsx"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,220p' reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/AudioTrack.tsx","rg -n \"AudioTrack|TimelineRenderer\" reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor -g '*test*'","cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/","nl -ba reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/AudioTrack.tsx | sed -n '1,120p'"]},{"task_id":"T10","status":"pending","executor_notes":"Final validation was executed in the required order. `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 22 files / 70 tests, and `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Structural checks also confirmed `useTimelineSave.ts` is 87 lines, `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, the legacy `applyTimelineEdit`/`applyResolvedConfigEdit` symbols no longer exist under `src/tools/video-editor`, and the Editor/Chrome/Playback context split remains. The final gate is still not satisfied because the full app suite `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts` reports multiple unrelated existing failures outside the video-editor refactor surface, and the literal hook-hierarchy target in the plan is not met yet: `useTimelineState` still composes the thin `useTimelineSave` shell rather than calling `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` directly.","files_changed":[".megaplan/plans/restructure-the-video-editor-20260406-0206/execution_batch_7.json"],"commands_run":["cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/","cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit","cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts","wc -l src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts","test -e src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts && echo exists || echo deleted","rg -n \"applyTimelineEdit|applyResolvedConfigEdit\" src/tools/video-editor","rg -n \"useTimelineCommit|useTimelinePersistence|usePollSync|useDerivedTimeline|useTimelineHistory|useRenderState|useAssetOperations|useEditorPreferences\" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts","rg -n \"TimelineEditorContext|TimelineChromeContext|TimelinePlaybackContext\" src/tools/video-editor -g '!**/*.test.*'"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC1","executor_note":"Confirmed. The audit identifies the full shared-ref surface across commit/edit, save/persist, and poll/sync (`dataRef`, `editSeqRef`, `savedSeqRef`, `pendingOpsRef`, `configVersionRef`, `lastSavedSignature`, `selectedClipIdRef`, `selectedTrackIdRef`, `isSavingRef`) and proposes a `scheduleSaveRef` bridge so the extracted modules can be composed without circular dependencies."},{"sense_check_id":"SC2","executor_note":"Verified with the full video-editor Vitest module suite and `npx tsc --noEmit`. `usePollSync.test.ts` exercises the full `editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving/pendingOps` matrix: when `isSaving=false`, the extracted gate matches the previous blacklist decisions; when `isSaving=true` and the other idle conditions are met, polls are intentionally rejected as `saving`, which is the approved default-reject tightening for this phase."},{"sense_check_id":"SC3","executor_note":"Confirmed. `useTimelineSave.ts` is 88 lines, composes `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync`, and still exports `SaveStatus`, `shouldAcceptPolledData`, and the same callable `useTimelineSave` surface used by `useTimelineData`. The full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` both passed, so no downstream consumer broke."},{"sense_check_id":"SC4","executor_note":"Confirmed. All video-editor callers now use `applyEdit`; `rg -n \"applyTimelineEdit|applyResolvedConfigEdit\" src/tools/video-editor` returns no remaining source usages. `createTrackAndMoveClip` still commits through the `type: 'config'` path in `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`, so its existing no-overlap-resolution semantics are preserved, and the old wrappers were removed from the commit/data/context surfaces."},{"sense_check_id":"SC5","executor_note":"Confirmed. The shared types now live in `useTimelineData.types.ts`, the planned importer sites now reference that module instead of `useTimelineData.ts`, and the old `ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData>` alias was replaced with the explicit `UseTimelineDataResult` type. `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed cleanly."},{"sense_check_id":"SC6","executor_note":"Confirmed. `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, `useTimelineState.ts` now owns the three orchestration pieces that previously lived in the wrapper (`onSaveSuccessRef` wiring to `render.setRenderDirty`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and the `useLayoutEffect` bridge from `history.onBeforeCommit` to `save.onBeforeCommitRef`), and both `useDerivedTimeline` calls remain: one for `save.selectedClipId`/`save.selectedTrackId` and one for `primaryClipId` multi-select derivation. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; the full video-editor Vitest module was also re-run and hit one unrelated existing failure in `ClipAction.test.tsx` outside this refactor path."},{"sense_check_id":"SC7","executor_note":"Confirmed. `resolveGroupOverlaps` now returns `metaPatches`, `applyMultiDragMoves` accumulates overlap patches across loop iterations with per-clip spread merge so existing `track` patches are preserved, and new direct tests were added for both `resolveOverlaps` and `resolveGroupOverlaps` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tests__/resolve-overlaps.test.ts`. The full video-editor Vitest module passed after the change."},{"sense_check_id":"SC8","executor_note":"Confirmed. `useDerivedTimeline` now derives `resolvedConfig` with `useMemo(() => data?.resolvedConfig ?? null, [data])`, and the targeted ESLint pass on the changed files completed cleanly, so the `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` rule is satisfied. The full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` both passed after the simplification; no automated regression surfaced that required reverting the change."},{"sense_check_id":"SC9","executor_note":"Confirmed. `AudioTrack` now keys each `Sequence` with `clip.id`, `clip.at`, `clip.from ?? 0`, `clip.to ?? ''`, and `clip.speed ?? 1`, and the file includes a comment explaining that Remotion audio timing and playback-rate changes require remount during playback."},{"sense_check_id":"SC10","executor_note":"No. The video-editor suite passes (22 files / 70 tests) and `npx tsc --noEmit` succeeds, but the full app suite still fails in multiple unrelated shared/tooling areas outside the video-editor refactor. The structural checks confirm `useTimelineSave.ts` is a thin 87-line composer, `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, the old edit-path symbols are gone, and the Editor/Chrome/Playback context split remains. The hook-hierarchy criterion is also not literally satisfied yet because `useTimelineState` still calls `useTimelineSave` rather than composing `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` directly."}]}
Running the final validation sequence for `T10` again from `reigh-app`, then I’ll verify the structural acceptance checks and write the batch checkpoint with only `T10` and `SC10` updates.
The video-editor suite is running. After that I’m re-running `tsc`, the full app suite, and then I’ll re-check the hook/composer invariants directly from source.

item.text: The video-editor suite passed cleanly on this rerun. Next is `tsc`, then the full app suite, and then I’ll record the final gate state with the structural checks.
The full app suite is running now. I’m letting it complete so the batch can report the actual gate result instead of a partial failure snapshot.

The full suite is still progressing past the earlier failures, which is what we need for the “re-run the full suite anyway” requirement. I’m waiting for the final exit so the report can cite the completed run rather than an interrupted one.
I already have enough failure evidence from the full app run. I’m checking the structural invariants now and then I’ll update the batch checkpoint file with only `T10` and `SC10`.
